groups.name: server.netty
groups.description: Configuration properties for the Netty-based web server that replaces Tomcat in this starter.

properties.name: server.netty.port
properties.description: TCP port the Netty server binds to. Use 0 to let the OS assign an ephemeral port (useful for tests).

properties.name: server.netty.boss-threads
properties.description: Number of threads in the boss event loop group that accepts incoming connections. One is typically sufficient.

properties.name: server.netty.worker-threads
properties.description: Number of threads in the worker event loop group that processes I/O for accepted connections. Use 0 to apply Netty's default of two times the number of available processors.

properties.name: server.netty.keep-alive
properties.description: Enables TCP SO_KEEPALIVE on accepted child channels. This is the socket-level keep-alive, independent of HTTP keep-alive semantics.

values.description: Bind to an ephemeral port chosen by the operating system.
